name: StudySapuri ENGLISH
overview: StudySapuri ENGLISH is Recruit's flagship Japanese-market English-learning subscription, anchoring the broader StudySapuri family. Three core consumer courses (New Daily Conversation 2,178 yen/mo, TOEIC L&R 3,278 yen/mo, Business English 3,278 yen/mo) wrap dictation, shadowing, AI pronunciation scoring, and a 'kamijugyo' (god-tier lecture) by celebrity instructor Masao Seki around a habit loop tuned for Japanese commuter learners. The brand reports a continuation rate of 91-92%, attributed to short 1-3 minute units, visualized study time, badges/streaks ('manarepo' parent-facing reports on the school side), and grandfathering of legacy 980 yen subscribers through the 2020 price hike. As of March 2026 it added a generative-AI two-way conversation mode across 800+ lessons.
originator_or_company: Recruit Co., Ltd. (Tokyo; CEO Hisayuki Idekoba). Originally launched 2015 as 'Eigo Sapuri' under Recruit Marketing Partners, consolidated into Recruit Holdings' StudySapuri family. Key product/design leaders: Yuki Ishiguro (Group Manager, Product Design / Design Manager — has been the public voice of StudySapuri ENGLISH UX since 2019). Star instructor: Masao Seki (TOEIC 990 perfect-score charisma teacher, headline talent for the brand). Scenario writers include scriptwriters from major Japanese TV dramas (Gokusen, Nurse no Oshigoto, Shitamachi Rocket).
key_resources: [Continuation Designed: StudySapuri's UX of habit (Recruit Tech Blog, Yuki Ishiguro)](https://techblog.recruit.co.jp/article-535/)
[Enhance Continuous Learning With StudySapuri (Spectrum Tokyo interview, Yuki Ishiguro)](https://spectrumtokyo.com/interviews/enhance-continuous-learning-with-studysapuri/)
[Why a price hike doubled members: Recruit's StudySapuri (President Online, 2022)](https://president.jp/articles/-/61274)
[Speakerdeck: 'Casually, fun, continuable' learning experience design (Ishiguro, Spectrum Tokyo Design Fest 2022)](https://speakerdeck.com/recruitengineers/spectrumtokyodesignfest2022_ishiguro)
[UX MILK Fest 2019: The role of design beyond UI in education services](https://blog.studysapuri.jp/entry/design-for-education)
core_mechanics: (1) MICRO-LESSON LOOP: every unit is designed to fit 1-3 minutes so that a commuter can complete a full input-output cycle on a single subway stop. Ishiguro frames this as 'lowering the activation energy' so missing a day requires almost zero willpower to recover. (2) INPUT-OUTPUT CYCLE: each lesson chains video lecture (kamijugyo by Masao Seki or scenario teachers) -> word-level vocabulary check -> dictation typing -> key-phrase repeat -> shadowing with playback (0.8x-1.2x speed) -> AI speech scoring (Excellent/Great/Good/Nice Try). (3) AI EVALUATION (2020+): proprietary speech-recognition + scoring AI rates utterance vs. reference on a 4-tier scale and shows a colored diff so learners can self-correct. Extended in 2022 with 'Basic Pronunciation Course' that uses the same engine for phoneme-level feedback. (4) TWO-WAY AI CONVERSATION (March 2026): generative AI partner that responds to free-form learner speech, gives natural-language feedback on vocabulary/grammar/phrasing, integrated across ~800 lessons. (5) PROGRESS VISUALIZATION: weekly study-time graph by day-of-week, settable weekly time goal, total study time counter, consecutive learning-days counter, '1-week challenge' badge. (6) WIDGETS (iOS home-screen): 'Continue from last', 'this week's study time', 'consecutive days', '1-minute quiz' — each is an explicit re-entry trigger. (7) BADGES: awarded for streak milestones, completed sections, dictation accuracy thresholds. (8) DRAMA-FORMAT SCRIPTED LESSONS: TV-drama scriptwriters author the dialogue arcs (e.g., New York-set storyline for New Daily Conversation, Shitamachi-Rocket-style storyline for Business English) so that learners are drawn back by narrative cliffhanger, not just XP. (9) MANAREPO (kids/school side): daily and weekly email digests of study time, lesson count, accuracy — a parent/teacher-facing report that surfaces quietly to the learner as a third-party gaze. (10) GRANDFATHER PRICING: when the consumer monthly went from 980 yen to 1,980 yen in Feb 2020, existing subscribers were locked at the old 980 yen rate as long as they did not cancel — a deliberate retention/loyalty mechanism. The 2024-06-25 terms revision again handled existing contracts under the prior clause until 2025-03-24, indicating a recurring pattern of soft transitions. (11) PRICE-LADDER: Basic monthly is the entry SKU; 6-month and 12-month prepay tiers reduce per-month cost (12-month is ~21% cheaper than monthly). Personal Coaching is a separate high-touch SKU at ~68,000 yen/3mo.
designer_or_researcher_voice: Yuki Ishiguro (Recruit, Group Manager Product Design): 'The accumulation of growth-realization (seichou-jikkan) and a sense of accomplishment becomes the very reason a learner wants to keep going.' (Recruit Tech Blog, Article 535)
Yuki Ishiguro: 'We strive to create a learning cycle that encompasses both input and output, aiming for retention.' (Spectrum Tokyo interview, 2022)
Yuki Ishiguro: 'Continuing means integrating learning into daily life as a cycle, not setting the bar too high, and visualizing the goals.' (Spectrum Tokyo)
Yuki Ishiguro on motivation collapse: 'When motivation drops, our job is to make the action easier — not to demand more willpower.' (UX MILK Fest 2019, paraphrased from Recruit Product Team Blog)

Yuki Ishiguro on competition: 'Our real competitor is not cram schools — it is LINE, smartphone games, and SNS. Anything that takes a moment of attention is competing for the same minute.' (UX MILK Fest 2019)

Ishiguro at Spectrum Tokyo Design Fest 2022: 'If it doesn't continue, it has no meaning. We tune three levers — meeting the user's expectation of the AI, calibrating the difficulty/time hurdle, and providing a stimulus when motivation dips.'
metrics_or_evidence: Continuation rate publicly cited at 91-92% across blog reviews and aggregator sites (e.g., StudyStudio, Tomigaoka, EnglishFactor). [uncertain] — these figures appear in marketing materials and partner sites; an audited primary-source disclosure was not located. Cumulative StudySapuri family users exceeded 13 million as of January 2023 (Recruit Holdings disclosure). StudySapuri paid-subscriber count reached approximately 1.57 million by March 2021 (Recruit IR). The 2020 price hike from 980 yen to 1,980 yen was followed by membership doubling rather than churn, per President Online's 2022 long-form analysis. Good Design Award winner in 2019 for the service experience. AI two-way conversation feature deployed across 800+ lessons in March 2026 (Recruit press release).
criticisms: (1) Self-management dependency: numerous user reviews flag that learners who lack self-discipline drop off because there is no live coach in the Basic plan; the system trades cost for personal accountability. (2) Speaking ceiling: the AI evaluator scores phrase-level accuracy but cannot replace human conversation; advanced learners (TOEIC 900+) report hitting a ceiling. (3) Mis-judged speech recognition: users report the AI sometimes mis-scores correct utterances, harming motivation. (4) Error explanations are thin: when a learner gets a question wrong, the app does not always explain WHY at the depth a tutor would. (5) Drama-narrative novelty wears off: scenario engagement decays once the story arc resolves — the input layer is finite where the drill layer is infinite. (6) Gamification limits (Ishiguro himself acknowledges): in the 2019 UX MILK talk he notes that extrinsic rewards become a 'double-edged sword' (Overjustification effect) — keeping the room clean turns into 'cleaning to get candy'. (7) Refund policy tightening (post 2025-08-27 contracts cannot get pro-rata refunds on long packs) drew complaints in consumer forums.
failure_cases: (1) The pre-2020 service was relatively flat-priced and engagement plateaued; Recruit responded with the 980->1980 yen price hike + scope expansion (more courses, AI features). (2) Old TOEIC course had correct/incorrect animations long enough that learners reported tempo loss; the team measured the latency was a churn driver and tightened the animation timing — surfaced in the Recruit Tech Blog as a small-stress example. (3) Non-grandfathered re-entry: users who canceled and rejoined post-Feb-2020 lost the 980 yen rate permanently, generating a recurring forum complaint thread. (4) Basic-plan support thinness: the team has had to repeatedly clarify that Q&A coaching is a paid Personal Coaching upsell, not part of Basic — a discoverability gap. (5) Long-pack mid-term refunds: the tightening on August 27, 2025 (no mid-cancel refunds on long packs except in narrow conditions) rolled back a previously consumer-friendly term, which drew negative reviews.
antipatterns: (1) Charging an existing loyal cohort the new price overnight without grandfathering — Recruit deliberately avoided this and that decision is widely credited with the post-hike retention. Skipping grandfathering is the antipattern, not embracing it. (2) Cartoon-mascot guilt notifications (Duolingo style) — StudySapuri ENGLISH avoids these in favor of calm second-person copy. Importing them would clash. (3) Hard paywall on the practice loop — StudySapuri ENGLISH uses a clean monthly subscription model rather than hearts/lives gating. Adding hearts would violate the brand's 'casually, fun, continuable' promise. (4) Pure XP optimization without input-output coupling — Ishiguro explicitly warns that 'cleaning the room becomes about earning candy'; gamifying without anchoring on real mastery causes Overjustification collapse. (5) Volume-escalation push notifications — StudySapuri uses widgets and gentle reminders, not high-frequency pings. Cranking up notification volume would exit the 'Less is more' lane.
applicability_to_shikaq: STRONG fit. StudySapuri ENGLISH and shikaq target a near-identical persona (20s/30s Japanese learners, often women, kyaria-up motivated, mobile-first) with a near-identical price ceiling (sub-2,000 yen monthly is the psychological line; shikaq's 980 yen sits below it). Translation playbook: (a) MICRO-LESSON 1-3 MIN: shikaq Practice and Lesson units must default to 3-minute targets to keep the cafe-iPad-1-handed persona inside one cup of latte. (b) INPUT-OUTPUT CHAIN: shikaq's video lesson -> dictation/key-phrase -> question drill chain mirrors StudySapuri's input-output design. For SPI/boki/IT-passport, the 'output' becomes solving a question and explaining the answer in own words. (c) AI EVALUATION: a generative-AI explainer/grader (DeepSeek-driven via Edge Functions) is the shikaq parallel to StudySapuri's two-way AI mode; 4-tier scoring scale (Excellent/Great/Good/Nice Try) is portable and calm. (d) MANAREPO -> WEEKLY DIGEST: shikaq weekly email/push digest of 'questions answered, accuracy trend, weak topics' as a self-coaching mirror — fits Apple Fitness Summary aesthetic. (e) WIDGETS: 'continue last lesson', 'this week study time', 'consecutive days', '1-minute quiz' as iOS Home Screen widgets — high-leverage re-entry trigger. (f) GRANDFATHER PRICING: when shikaq raises the 980 yen price (likely necessary later), grandfather day-1 cohort indefinitely — direct copy of Recruit's 2020 playbook, perfectly consistent with the brand's quiet, customer-respecting tone. (g) HEXAD coverage: Free Spirit (custom weekly time goal), Achiever (badges + accuracy ranks), Philanthropist (light study-group invite for SPI/boki), Player kept minimal. (h) DRAMA-FORMAT inspiration: for ITパスポート and 簿記2, frame core scenarios as short 'workplace drama' vignettes (e.g., 簿記 = small-cafe owner closing the books) so the input layer has narrative pull, not just facts.
phase_assignment: Phase 1 (3 weeks): micro-lesson 1-3 min default, weekly study-time graph on Today, consecutive-days counter, gentle daily reminder copy in Apple HIG voice. Phase 2 (4-6 weeks): AI evaluation/explainer for written-answer questions (DeepSeek via Edge Function), 4-tier rank UI, badges, iOS Home Screen widgets. Phase 3 (6-8 weeks): Two-way AI 'tutor chat' for SPI verbal / boki story problems, weekly email digest (manarepo equivalent), grandfather-pricing infrastructure ready before any price change. Rationale: micro-lesson + study-time visualization is the same Phase-1 'CURR primitive' as Duolingo's streak — earliest ROI; AI features benefit from having the loop and data flowing first.
integration_points: Sidebar: small 'consecutive days' indicator (flame.fill SF Symbol) near profile, identical placement to existing avatar slot. TodayContent: weekly time-by-day-of-week bar graph component (extension of ActivityRings) + 'Continue last lesson' card at top. Practice screen: enforce 1-3 minute session targets via lesson chunking; add 4-tier rank chip on the answer-grading state. New DB tables: study_sessions (user_id, lesson_id, started_at, ended_at, minutes, correct_count) for the time graph; ai_feedback (session_id, prompt, response, rank, feedback_text). Reuse: progress (already in db-schema.md), quiz_results, profiles. Edge Functions: nightly-digest (manarepo-style email + push), ai-grade (DeepSeek call). iOS Home Screen widgets via Expo Widget extensions (post-SDK 55) for 'Continue', 'This week', 'Consecutive days', '1-minute quiz'. Pricing infrastructure: subscriptions table extension with grandfather_price_jpy nullable column to lock legacy cohorts at 980 yen.
risks: (1) Brand collision: StudySapuri ENGLISH owns the kamijugyo + drama-narrative + Inada-aesthetic format; shikaq must avoid copying the visual idiom (its blue-white education-service look is the explicit anti-pattern in design-principles.md). Borrow mechanics, not aesthetics. (2) AI cost ceiling: two-way generative AI conversation across 800 lessons assumes Recruit's scale; shikaq at 980 yen ARPU must throttle AI usage per user per month or risk negative gross margin. (3) Scope creep: matching StudySapuri's full feature surface (drama scripts, celebrity teacher, AI conversation, widgets, manarepo email, grandfather logic) is a 9-12 month effort — must phase strictly. (4) Self-management failure mode: same as the StudySapuri criticism — Basic-plan-only learners drop off; shikaq has no equivalent of Personal Coaching upsell yet. (5) Refund-policy mistake: Recruit's August 2025 tightening attracted complaints; shikaq should design refund terms day one to avoid a similar one-way-rachet PR moment. (6) AI grading mis-judgment hurts motivation; need calibrated thresholds and human-review escape hatch.
apple_hig_compatibility: compatible. StudySapuri ENGLISH's underlying mechanics — micro lessons, calm progress visualization, second-person tone, badges as quiet rewards, widget-based re-entry, grandfather pricing as customer respect — all map cleanly onto Apple HIG Foundations (Clarity / Deference / Less but better). The brand's actual rendered UI is closer to a generic 'kyouiku-app' style (blue/white, rounded gradient cards) which shikaq would not import; but the BEHAVIORAL design layer is HIG-compatible. SF Symbols and Liquid Glass can carry the same loop without aesthetic conflict.
ethical_score: positive-leaning-neutral. Center for Humane Technology rubric: low compulsion stack (no hearts/lives, no leagues by default, no high-frequency pings), explicit 'lower the bar' design philosophy from Ishiguro, grandfather pricing signals customer respect over extraction. Mild concerns: (a) self-management failure leaves Basic users stranded — borderline 'placebo learning' for the unmotivated; (b) AI grading errors can demotivate; (c) refund tightening 2025-08-27 is a small ethical regression. Net: this is the rare case study that aligns with the Humane Technology framework rather than fighting it.
evidence_quality: company_case_study. Continuation rate of 91-92% is widely cited but appears in marketing/partner contexts rather than peer-reviewed disclosure [uncertain]. Designer voice is solid (multiple primary-source talks and blog posts by Ishiguro). Press releases for AI features are primary-source. Cumulative-user counts come from Recruit IR. Academic RCT-grade evidence on the specific causal mechanics is absent.
localization_fit_japan: high. StudySapuri ENGLISH is built for the Japanese market by a Japanese team and is the dominant English-learning app in Japan. The 1-3 minute commute-friendly cadence, calm second-person tone, drama-narrative input format, NHK-radio-style daily-habit framing, and the 980 yen psychological ceiling all encode strong Japan-market product-market fit. Specifically for the shikaq persona (20s women, kyaria-up motivated, cafe-iPad), the product's quiet aesthetics and absence of leaderboard-shaming match Japanese cultural preference for private progress over public ranking. Manarepo (parent/self-facing weekly digest) maps onto Japan's strong self-improvement-journal culture (techo, hobonichi). Grandfather pricing maps onto Japan's heavy customer-loyalty/long-customer-relationship norms (e.g., Mizuho, Suica grandfathering patterns).
